module.name: sceasyquake
module.description: SeisBench-based ML phase picker for SeisComP. Reads waveforms from the local SeedLink server, runs a deep-learning phase detection model (PhaseNet, GPD, or EQTransformer) and publishes P and S picks to the PICK messaging group.

group.name: seedlink
group.description: SeedLink server from which waveforms are read. Normally this is the local SeisComP seedlink service.

parameter.name: host
parameter.description: Hostname or IP of the SeedLink server.

parameter.name: port
parameter.description: TCP port of the SeedLink server.

parameter.name: streams.codes
parameter.description: Comma-separated list of waveform stream selectors in NET.STA.LOC.CHA format (wildcards accepted). Example: CI.*.*.HHZ,CI.*.*.EHZ Leave empty to subscribe to all vertical channels (*.*..??Z).

group.name: picker
group.description:
Settings for the ML phase picker.
There are two execution backends:
seisbench – Uses the SeisBench library (PyTorch-based). Supports all three architectures (PhaseNet, GPD, EQTransformer) and accepts custom model weights via picker.model_path.
easyquake – Uses the bundled easyQuake TF/Keras wrappers. Supports PhaseNet and GPD only. Always runs with the built-in pretrained weights; picker.model_path is ignored.
Select the backend with picker.backend, then choose the model architecture with picker.model.

parameter.name: backend
parameter.description:
Execution backend for ML inference:
seisbench – SeisBench (PyTorch). Supports PhaseNet, GPD, and EQTransformer. Custom weights can be loaded via picker.model_path. easyquake – easyQuake TF/Keras wrappers. Supports PhaseNet and GPD only. Uses built-in pretrained weights (picker.model_path has no effect). stub – No-op stub; useful for testing the messaging pipeline without real picks.

parameter.name: model
parameter.description:
Model architecture to use for phase detection.
phasenet – PhaseNet (Zhu & Beroza 2019). Available for both seisbench and easyquake backends. eqtransformer – EQTransformer (Mousavi et al. 2020). Only available with the seisbench backend. gpd – Generalized Phase Detection (Ross et al. 2018). Available for both seisbench and easyquake backends. Uses a higher default threshold (picker.gpd_threshold = 0.994).
For the easyquake backend, eqtransformer is not supported and will fall back to phasenet.

parameter.name: pretrained
parameter.description:
Base architecture / pretrained weight set (seisbench backend only).
This parameter has two roles depending on whether picker.model_path is set:
• No model_path (standard pretrained weights): Names the weight set downloaded from the SeisBench model hub and used directly for inference.
• With model_path pointing to a raw .pt/.pth state-dict file: Names the base architecture template used to construct the model object (norm mode, label layout, sampling rate, etc.) before the custom weights are loaded. The pretrained weights themselves are immediately replaced by the file in model_path. Set this to the weight set whose architecture most closely matches how your custom model was trained.
• With model_path pointing to a SeisBench directory (contains .pt + .json): This parameter is ignored entirely; all architecture metadata is read from the .json file.
Common values by architecture: phasenet : stead (default), diting, ethz, scedc, geofon gpd : original (default), scedc, geofon eqtransformer: original (default), ethz, scedc, stead

parameter.name: model_path
parameter.description:
Path to custom model weights (seisbench backend only). Leave empty to use the hub weights named by picker.pretrained.
Accepted formats: • SeisBench save directory containing a matching .pt weights file and .json metadata file. All architecture settings come from the .json; picker.pretrained is ignored. • Raw PyTorch state-dict file (.pt / .pth). The model object is first constructed from picker.pretrained (as a template for architecture/norm/labels), then the weights are replaced by this file. Set picker.pretrained to the weight set whose architecture matches your training setup (e.g. stead for a PhaseNet model trained on STEAD-style normalisation).
Has no effect when picker.backend = easyquake.

parameter.name: label_order
parameter.description:
Output channel order the model was trained with (seisbench backend only). Leave empty for standard pretrained weights.
SeisBench PhaseNet uses NPS order by default. If a custom model was trained with a different output order (e.g. PSN), set this parameter so the output weights are reordered. Example: PSN (P=channel 0, S=channel 1, Noise=channel 2)

parameter.name: threshold
parameter.description: Minimum probability required to report a pick. Applies to all phases unless overridden by picker.p_threshold or picker.s_threshold. Range: 0 – 1.

parameter.name: p_threshold
parameter.description: Minimum probability threshold for P picks. Overrides picker.threshold for the P output channel. Range: 0 – 1.

parameter.name: s_threshold
parameter.description: Minimum probability threshold for S picks. Overrides picker.threshold for the S output channel. Range: 0 – 1.

parameter.name: min_distance
parameter.description: Minimum time separation in seconds between consecutive picks of the same phase on one channel.

parameter.name: norm
parameter.description: Amplitude normalisation mode for the SeisBench model. Leave empty to use the model's built-in default. peak – peak-amplitude normalisation (required for models trained with sbg.Normalize(amp_norm_type='peak')). std – standard-deviation normalisation.

parameter.name: gpd_threshold
parameter.description: Detection probability threshold for GPD picks. Only used when picker.backend = gpd. The original easyQuake pipeline uses 0.994.

parameter.name: device
parameter.description: Compute device for model inference. cpu – CPU inference (always available). cuda – GPU inference via CUDA (requires an NVIDIA GPU with compatible drivers and pytorch-cuda installed).

parameter.name: buffer_seconds
parameter.description: Length of the sliding waveform window passed to the model. Longer buffers provide more context but increase latency.

parameter.name: step_seconds
parameter.description: How far the window advances between inference calls. Smaller values reduce pick latency but increase GPU/CPU load.

command-line.description: sceasyquake continuously reads waveforms, runs ML inference, and publishes Phase picks to the SeisComP messaging bus.
